deeperExplanation: SQLite supports ACID transactions. Statements execute within a transaction block. Always call `conn.commit()` to persist changes, or `conn.rollback()` on failure.
internalImplementation: CPython wraps SQLite C C-API library (`libsqlite3`).

examples.code:
with sqlite3.connect('app.db') as conn:
    # Automatically commits on exit or rolls back on exception!
    conn.execute('INSERT INTO users VALUES (2, "Bob")')

performanceConsiderations.timeComplexity: Indexed queries O(log N), unindexed full table scans O(N).

bestPractices: Use connection as context manager for transaction safety.
